response.justification: Wei Chen has 15+ years of broad software engineering experience and entrepreneurial exposure through GISCollective, but falls well short of what a Director-level AI strategy IC requires. Their only AI-adjacent work is very recent MCP tooling at re:cinq (2025), with no history of AI/ML system design, no advanced degree, and no evidence of customer advisory work, cross-organizational technical influence, or recognized AI thought leadership. The profile reads as a strong senior/staff IC, not a principal-level AI strategist.
strengths: 15+ years of polyglot engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains satisfies the baseline experience requirement
Founding and sustained ownership of GISCollective demonstrates genuine entrepreneurial drive and open-source platform building
Current role includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework work, showing directional interest in the target domain
concerns: AI involvement is shallow and recent (2025 only); the role demands a track record of shipping recognized AI-powered products or platforms
No evidence of Director-level scope: no team leadership, no cross-org influence, no strategic customer advisory or partnership engagements
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and no publications, keynotes, or industry recognition that would support a principal/strategic advisory role

key_factors.factor: Absence of credible AI expertise depth
No director-level leadership or influence track record
Broad engineering background and entrepreneurial experience
